q: They walked besides each other in silence.
His relation to Kalpana is good.
The teacher has not control upon the students.
We travelled with steamer to reach there.
His statement is very much similar about yours.
The shopkeeper deals with grain but did not deal honestly with me.
correction: deals with grain → deals in grain

q: He appealed to the judge for his release from jail.
I complained against the magistrate against Munna about his misconduct.
We are responsible for God for our actions.
The doctor referred the patient for the P.M.C.H. without examining him.
correction: for the P.M.C.H. → to the P.M.C.H.

q: My cousin has been married with the richest man of the village.
He was shocked to hear that her mother died of an accident.
correction: died of an accident → died in an accident

q: Mr. Kisori Takur is blind with one eye.
correction: with one eye → in one eye

q: On a holiday I prefer writing than going out visiting friends.
correction: than going out → to going out

q: I have been working on the problem from a long time but is still not able to solve it.
options: the problem from a long time
correction: from a long time → for a long time

q: Mr. Mishra and his wife were invited for the cultural function at my house.
correction: for the cultural function → to the cultural function

q: Beside chocolate they also bought many balloons for the child.
Yesterday, Binay came across with one of his old friends.
Of what I know of her I hesitate to trust in him.
correction: Of what I know → From what I know

q: He left to Delhi this morning for some official work.
correction: left to Delhi → left for Delhi

q: He cannot prevent me to go there.
My book has been missing from my room till yesterday.
Akbar came on the throne at a very early age.
correction: came on the throne → came to the throne

q: You must provide to your children against the evil day.
correction: to your children → for your children

q: I will send you this book latest by 10th February.
They all met in a room which was close to the centre to discuss about the plan of action.
options: They all met in a room
which was close to the centre
to discuss about the plan of action.

q: When the police accused him with theft, nobody believed them, as he was well known for his honesty.
options: When the police accused him with theft,
as he was well known for his honesty.
correction: accused him with → accused him of

q: The meeting scheduled to be held between 2 P.M. to 3 P.M. was postponed suddenly.
options: The meeting scheduled to be held
correction: between 2 P.M. to 3 P.M. → from 2 P.M. to 3 P.M.

q: He was conferred with an honorary D. Litt. degree in recognition of his services to the cause of learning.
options: He was conferred with an honorary D. Litt. degree

q: The myth that some races are naturally superior than others has now been fully demolished.
This particular issue has been hanging in fire for a long time.
correction: hanging in fire → hanging in the fire

q: In the past people travelled by foot.
You should know that the ultimate decision in the matters rests upon the board of directors.
options: that the ultimate decision in the matters
rests upon the board of directors.

q: In some tribal areas people worship snakes and are not scared from them.
options: and are not scared from them.

q: His new proposal seems hardly different than that the one I've just rejected.
options: than that the one I've just rejected.

q: To lead a well-balanced life you shall have to cultivate other interests beside your professional ones.
options: you shall have to cultivate other interests
correction: beside your professional ones → besides your professional ones

q: After nagging constantly for months and administering weekly tests the teacher was at least pleased for the boys' progress.
options: the teacher was at least pleased for the boys' progress.

q: I'd appreciate spending half an hour with you to discuss about how these and other experience might be mutually beneficial.
options: I'd appreciate spending half an hour with you
to discuss about how these and other experience

q: The teacher was happy when he entered the class which comprised of a hundred students.
options: which comprised of a hundred students.

q: Yet, firms and other concerns are spending large sums of money all the time and somehow, we take this for granted and never stop to wonder where it comes from.
options: Yet, firms and other concerns are spending large sums of money all the time
and somehow, we take this for granted
and never stop to wonder where it comes from.

q: Thank for your invitation for lunch which I am very pleased to accept.
options: Thank for your invitation for lunch

q: Both the boys came late in the hall and sat besides me.
correction: in the hall → to the hall

q: I signed on the receipt in the morning but the pay has not been disbursed to me.
options: in the morning but the pay
has not been disbursed to me.
correction: signed on the receipt → signed the receipt

q: In my opinion a pencil is always preferable than a pen.
You must make up the time you wasted this afternoon by working late over to night.
options: by working late over to night.
correction: working late over to night → working late tonight

q: The extraordinary ability of the satellites to see, listen and communicate has made them integral with modern warfare and nuclear war strategies in particular.
options: The extraordinary ability of the satellites to
see, listen and communicate has made them integral with
modern warfare and nuclear war strategies in particular.

q: He looked like a lion baulked from its prey.
Often in political campaigns, a point is reached at which the candidates take out their gloves and start slagging with bare fists.
options: Often in political campaigns, a point is reached at which
the candidates take out their gloves
and start slagging with bare fists.

q: One of the major aims of the Air Force was the complete demolition of all means of transportation by the bombing of rail lines and terminals.
options: One of the major aims of the Air Force
was the complete demolition of all means of transportation
by the bombing of rail lines and terminals.

q: The celebrated singer was surrounded by the usual crowd of lackeys and hangers-in.
The aircraft was overloaded there was something wrong of the battery and the engine was making a queer noise.
options: there was something wrong of the battery
and the engine was making a queer noise.
correction: wrong of the battery → wrong with the battery

q: I warned him for the danger he was going to face during the hiking.
correction: for the danger → about the danger

q: Ordinarily, when in difficulty Renuka prefers keeping her counsel than running about taking advice.
correction: than running about → rather than running about

q: Sheeila advised to her child not to play with the ball on the road.
Basu was trying to pass through the gap on the fence when he tore his shirt.
correction: through the gap on → through the gap in

q: I have been teaching in this school since several years but have never met such a hopeless class as this.
options: I have been teaching in this school
such a hopeless class as this.
correction: since several years → for several years

q: It is easy to see that a lawyer's demeanour in court may be prejudicial against the interests of his client.
options: It is easy to see that
may be prejudicial against the interests of his client.

q: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax is due to fact that we did not receive pay on time.
options: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax
that we did not receive pay on time.
correction: due to fact → due to the fact

q: The policemen fired all the students when they were attacked by some of them.
correction: fired all the students → fired at all the students

q: He was not promoted to the rank of colonel till for a few months of his resignation.
correction: till for a few → until a few

q: All the players except Sam were present into the field.
correction: into the field → on the field

q: Despite of a good monsoon this year, the production of food grains in the country did not go up.
correction: Despite of a good monsoon → Despite a good monsoon

q: Yesterday, while crossing the road he was run out by a truck.
correction: run out by → run over by

q: The girl wrote an essay so well that her teacher was exceedingly pleased at her.
correction: pleased at her → pleased with her

q: He tried to open the cane by a cane opener.
correction: to open the cane → to open the can

q: When the students reached late the teacher objected their entering the class without his permission.
options: the teacher objected their entering the class

q: The captain and his wife were invited for the cultural function at my home.
correction: for the cultural → to the cultural

q: The government decided to write of the debts of the farmers who were below the poverty line.
correction: write of the debts → write off the debts

q: Children visiting the park are amused by the monkeys play in the cages.
correction: play in the cages → playing in the cages

q: The great actor was angry with the treatment during the party in Ashoka Hall.
correction: during the party → at the party

q: Don't go by his appearance, he is capable at doing any work without any hesitation.
The teacher pointed out the students that they might learn something from Kalidas because Kalidas was the Shakespeare of India.
options: Kalidas was the Shakespeare of India.

q: The consequence of the action was not fully realised to the authorities.
options: the action was not fully realised
correction: to the authorities → by the authorities

q: I am pleased to sanction one special increment to all the employees with the month.
correction: with the month → for the month

q: I have an appointment on the 9th of September on 5 o'clock.
